steps.title: Use the tangent point
steps.content: A horizontal line intersects this downward-opening parabola at exactly one point only when it passes through the vertex. The line $2y=c$ has constant $y$-value $\frac{c}{2}$, so find the vertex height of the parabola.

steps.title: Convert the height to c
steps.content:
Substitute the vertex input into the parabola.
$$y=-2(\frac{11}{4})^2+11(\frac{11}{4})$$
$$y=-\frac{121}{8}+\frac{242}{8}=\frac{121}{8}$$
Since $2y=c$,
$$c=2(\frac{121}{8})=\frac{121}{4}$$
The answer is 30.25.
